categories.name: Research Infrastructure
categories.description: Artifact structure, output format, schema compliance

categories.name: Research State Context
categories.description: Validates what context is present in agent input/output state

categories.name: Research Behavioral
categories.description: Core agent behaviors — actions the agent must perform

categories.name: Research Quality
categories.description: Quality of research outputs, scored by LLM-as-judge

categories.name: Research Reasoning
categories.description: Agent thought process, reflection quality, decision-making

categories.name: Research Integration
categories.description: Handoff quality, spec-writer compatibility, feedback loops

evals.title: Research bundle exists at correct path
evals.description: Verify research bundle artifact exists at artifacts/research/research-bundle.md
evals.pass_criteria: File exists at canonical path

evals.title: Research bundle has valid YAML frontmatter
evals.description: Verify research bundle has YAML frontmatter with required fields: artifact, project_id, title, version, status, stage, authors, lineage
evals.pass_criteria: All required frontmatter fields present and valid

evals.title: Research bundle schema completeness and quality
evals.description: Assess whether the research bundle contains all required sections with substantive content

anchors.1: Research bundle is missing entirely or is a stub with no meaningful sections; structure is unrecognizable as a research artifact
anchors.2: Some sections present but major required sections missing (e.g., no Ecosystem Options or no Model Capabilities); sections that exist are shallow placeholders
anchors.3: All required sections present but some are thin or lack detail; structure is correct but content doesn't fully populate the schema
anchors.4: All required sections present and substantively filled; minor gaps in optional subsections or inconsistent depth across sections
anchors.5: Every section is richly populated with structured content; sections are internally consistent, well-organized with clear subsections, and the schema feels purpose-built for the spec-writer's needs

evals.title: Citation quality across all findings
evals.description: Assess structural and presentational quality of citations in the research bundle

anchors.1: No citations whatsoever; findings are presented as unsourced assertions; completely failed to cite anything
anchors.2: Some citations exist but are vague or generic (e.g., 'from LangChain docs' with no URL/page); many findings remain uncited; source types not distinguished
anchors.3: Most findings have citations with URLs or references, but citations lack specificity (e.g., links to homepage not specific page); no relationship-building between sources
anchors.4: Findings cite specific pages, API references, or tweet URLs; source types are clearly distinguished (docs vs. tweet vs. API ref); citations are traceable and verifiable
anchors.5: Citations are precise and contextualized — e.g., 'Harrison Chase stated [X] on 2025-06-15, which aligns with the approach described in the LangGraph persistence docs [URL], further validated by Anthropic's tool-use research [URL]'; agent builds explicit relationships between sources, demonstrating genuine internalization of the material

evals.title: PRD content present in agent input state
evals.description: Verify PRD file path or content is available in agent's input state
evals.pass_criteria: prd_path or prd_content exists in input state

evals.title: Eval suite content present in agent input state
evals.description: Verify eval suite file path or content is available in agent's input state
evals.pass_criteria: eval_suite_path or eval_suite_content exists in input state

evals.title: Project config present in state
evals.description: Verify Twitter handles, skills paths, and agent config are in input state
evals.pass_criteria: twitter_handles, skills_paths, and config exist in input state

evals.title: Research bundle written to agent output state
evals.description: Verify research bundle path or content is in agent's output state after completion
evals.pass_criteria: research_bundle_path exists in output state

evals.title: Agent reads full PRD before starting research
evals.description: Verify agent reads entire PRD file, not just first 100 lines
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows multiple read_file calls on PRD path covering full file length

evals.title: Agent reads eval suite before starting research
evals.description: Verify agent reads the eval suite artifact
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows read_file call on eval suite path

evals.title: Agent persists decomposition file
evals.description: Verify agent creates a PRD decomposition file at artifacts/research/research-decomposition.md
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows write_file call to research-decomposition.md

evals.title: Agent uses web_search/web_fetch tools
evals.description: Verify agent performs web research using search and fetch tools
evals.pass_criteria: Trace contains web_search and/or web_fetch tool calls

evals.title: No hallucinated sources
evals.description: Cross-reference every URL cited in research bundle against web_fetch calls in trace AND re-fetch for content accuracy
evals.pass_criteria: Every cited URL appears in trace as a web_fetch call AND re-fetched content matches agent's representation

evals.title: Agent researches Anthropic model capabilities
evals.description: Verify agent conducts research on Anthropic model capabilities
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows web_search/web_fetch calls to Anthropic docs or related content

evals.title: Agent uses skills directory
evals.description: Verify agent reads files from /skills/ directory
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows read_file calls to paths under /skills/

evals.title: Agent spawns sub-agents for research
evals.description: Verify agent delegates research to sub-agents via the task tool
evals.pass_criteria: Trace contains task tool calls with research-related descriptions

evals.title: Sub-agents execute in parallel
evals.description: Verify multiple sub-agents are launched concurrently, not sequentially
evals.pass_criteria: Multiple task tool calls appear in the same tool_use block or with overlapping timestamps

evals.title: Sub-agent findings are aggregated
evals.description: Verify main agent reads sub-agent output files after sub-agents complete
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows read_file calls to sub-findings directory after task calls complete

evals.title: HITL gate fires before deep-dive research
evals.description: Verify agent presents HITL research cluster for approval before deep-dive verification
evals.pass_criteria: Trace shows request_approval or HITL interrupt before deep-dive web_fetch calls

evals.title: PRD decomposition quality
evals.description: Assess how thoroughly the agent decomposed the PRD into research topics with line citations

anchors.1: Agent did not read the PRD or produced research unrelated to PRD requirements; no decomposition file created
anchors.2: Agent created a decomposition file but it only captures 1-2 high-level topics; major PRD requirement areas ignored; no PRD line references
anchors.3: Decomposition file covers most PRD topics but misses non-obvious dependencies, eval implications, or cross-cutting concerns; PRD line references present but incomplete
anchors.4: Decomposition file thoroughly maps PRD requirements to research topics with PRD line citations; minor gaps in cross-cutting concerns or eval-driven research needs; agent uses the file to track progress
anchors.5: Decomposition file systematically maps EVERY PRD requirement + eval to specific research topics with exact PRD line citations; identifies cross-cutting concerns and architectural dependencies; prioritized by impact; agent actively references and updates the file as research progresses

evals.title: Research breadth
evals.description: Assess whether the agent found ALL relevant ecosystem options with zero blind spots

anchors.1: Agent researched only 1-2 obvious approaches; entire categories of relevant ecosystem options are missing; major blind spots in coverage
anchors.2: Agent covered the most obvious options but missed significant alternatives; research is narrow within each topic area; didn't explore adjacent or emerging approaches
anchors.3: Agent found most mainstream options across required topic areas, but missed niche alternatives, recent releases, or community-contributed approaches
anchors.4: Agent systematically explored all major ecosystem options across every PRD requirement area; found both mainstream and less-obvious alternatives; minor gaps limited to very new or obscure options
anchors.5: Exhaustive coverage — agent found every relevant option including emerging/experimental approaches, community forks, and alternatives from adjacent ecosystems; explicitly flagged areas where options are limited

evals.title: Research depth
evals.description: Assess whether the agent went deep on promising approaches (source code, architecture, not just READMEs)

anchors.1: Research is entirely surface-level — agent only read READMEs, landing pages, or top-level docs; no architectural understanding demonstrated
anchors.2: Agent went slightly beyond surface (e.g., getting-started guides) but didn't examine architectural patterns, source code, or advanced usage
anchors.3: Moderate depth — examined docs, some API references, maybe a tutorial — but didn't dig into implementation details or trade-offs
anchors.4: Agent went deep on promising approaches — read API references, examined source code or architecture docs, identified trade-offs and limitations
anchors.5: Agent went to the bottom of the rabbit hole — examined source code, understood internal architecture, identified undocumented behaviors, found real-world usage patterns from issues/PRs/discussions

evals.title: Citation accuracy (content re-fetch verified)
evals.description: Assess whether cited content accurately represents what the source actually says, verified via re-fetching

anchors.1: Citations are fabricated — URLs don't exist, content described doesn't match what the source actually says, or source pages return 404
anchors.2: Citations point to real URLs but content is significantly misrepresented — agent paraphrased incorrectly, attributed statements to wrong sources, or drew unsupported conclusions
anchors.3: Citations are real and content is roughly accurate, but key details are wrong or important caveats are omitted
anchors.4: Citations are accurate — re-fetched content confirms the agent's representation; source types correctly identified; minor imprecisions in paraphrasing
anchors.5: Citations are precisely accurate — every claim maps exactly to source content upon re-fetch; direct quotes are verbatim; paraphrases faithfully represent the source; nuances and caveats preserved

evals.title: Research bundle utility for spec-writer
evals.description: Assess whether a spec-writer could make all architectural decisions from this bundle alone

anchors.1: Bundle is unusable — findings are unstructured, lack context, or don't map to PRD requirements; spec-writer would redo research from scratch
anchors.2: Bundle contains some useful information but is poorly organized, missing comparative analysis; spec-writer needs significant supplementary research
anchors.3: Bundle is structured and covers main topics, but trade-off analysis is shallow and spec-writer needs additional research on key decisions
anchors.4: Bundle provides clear ecosystem options with trade-offs, model capabilities mapped to requirements, and enough detail for confident architectural decisions; minor gaps on edge cases
anchors.5: Bundle is a complete decision-support document — every PRD requirement has researched options with explicit trade-offs, evidence-backed recommendations, and the spec-writer can proceed without any additional research

evals.title: Twitter/SME consultation quality
evals.description: Assess quality of SME perspective gathering and integration

anchors.1: Agent never consulted the provided Twitter handles; SME perspectives completely absent; or agent fabricated tweets
anchors.2: Agent fetched some tweets but only surface-level — grabbed recent tweets without assessing relevance; no analysis of SME perspectives
anchors.3: Agent found relevant tweets/posts and included them as data points, but didn't connect SME perspectives to docs/skills findings
anchors.4: Agent systematically consulted specified SMEs, identified relevant content, and analyzed how perspectives validate or challenge approaches found in docs
anchors.5: Agent deeply integrated SME perspectives — contextualized tweets within broader research, used SME insights to prioritize options, wove SME voices into the narrative

evals.title: Skill trigger relevance and timing
evals.description: Assess whether the agent triggered the right skills at the right time

anchors.1: Agent never triggered any skills despite them being available and relevant; or triggered completely irrelevant skills
anchors.2: Agent triggered skills but at wrong times — e.g., after already completing research on that topic, or triggered broadly without targeting specific PRD topics
anchors.3: Agent triggered contextually appropriate skills for major topics, but missed opportunities where a specific skill would have directly answered a PRD requirement
anchors.4: Agent triggered the right skills at the right time for most topics; skill access clearly driven by current research question; minor missed opportunities
anchors.5: Agent strategically triggers specific skills precisely when most relevant — e.g., accesses LangGraph persistence skills exactly when decomposing the PRD's state-management requirement

evals.title: Skill reflection and internalization quality
evals.description: Assess whether agent read skills deeply, reflected, and internalized as baseline knowledge

anchors.1: Agent reads skill content but shows no evidence of processing it; skill output is ignored or copy-pasted verbatim; or agent only skimmed first few lines
anchors.2: Agent reads portions of skill files and acknowledges content superficially but clearly didn't read deep enough to find substantive information
anchors.3: Agent reads skill files with reasonable depth and extracts key takeaways, but reflection is generic — doesn't connect findings to specific PRD requirements
anchors.4: Agent reads skill files thoroughly, identifies actionable content, reflects on how it maps to PRD needs, and treats skill findings as baseline knowledge; minor gaps in cross-referencing
anchors.5: Agent reads skill files exhaustively, synthesizes across multiple skills to build coherent baseline understanding, and explicitly articulates what it now knows vs. what still needs web research

evals.title: Skill-to-research-decision influence
evals.description: Assess whether skill findings actually influenced the agent's research direction

anchors.1: Skills had zero observable influence on research direction; research would have been identical without skills
anchors.2: Agent mentions skill findings but research direction doesn't change — continues pre-determined path regardless
anchors.3: Skills noticeably influence some decisions — e.g., agent follows up on a library mentioned in a skill — but influence is limited to obvious leads
anchors.4: Skills clearly shape research strategy — agent adjusts what to investigate based on skill findings, drops dead ends skills revealed, deepens validated approaches
anchors.5: Skills are a primary driver of research strategy — agent uses findings to dynamically reprioritize, identifies non-obvious connections skills surfaced, and traces how specific skill insights led to specific decisions

evals.title: Sub-agent task delegation quality
evals.description: Assess quality of sub-agent topology reasoning and task brief specificity

anchors.1: Agent didn't delegate, or delegated with no meaningful task description and no reasoning about topology
anchors.2: Agent delegated but used rigid/mechanical split without reasoning about whether grouping makes sense; some tasks too thin, others too broad
anchors.3: Agent created reasonable tasks with some rationale, but didn't consider compute efficiency, dependencies, or volume differences between domains
anchors.4: Agent dynamically reasoned about delegation — considered volume per domain, grouped logically, explained rationale; task briefs have specific research questions; minor suboptimal grouping
anchors.5: Sophisticated metacognitive reasoning — explicitly evaluated multiple topology options, considered compute cost, domain dependencies, breadth vs depth; each sub-agent brief reflects awareness of what others are covering

evals.title: Research findings synthesis quality
evals.description: Assess how well the agent synthesized sub-agent findings into a coherent bundle

anchors.1: No synthesis — sub-agent findings dumped as-is with no narrative; contradictions unresolved; reads like disconnected reports
anchors.2: Minimal synthesis — merged outputs but didn't resolve contradictions or identify cross-cutting patterns; organized by sub-agent not by topic
anchors.3: Moderate synthesis — reorganized by topic and created readable structure, but missed cross-cutting patterns and didn't reconcile conflicting findings
anchors.4: Strong synthesis — integrated findings into coherent bundle organized around PRD requirements; cross-cutting patterns identified; contradictions resolved with reasoning
anchors.5: Masterful synthesis — unified narrative where sections build on previous ones; explicit cross-references; contradictions explained; emergent insights no single sub-agent could have produced

evals.title: HITL research cluster quality
evals.description: Assess quality of HITL cluster presentation — grouping, rationale, and specificity

anchors.1: No HITL presented; or content is empty/meaningless
anchors.2: HITL fires but cluster is poorly formed — targets are vague, no rationale, illogical grouping
anchors.3: HITL presents reasonable cluster with target list, but rationale is thin — doesn't explain why these targets were selected
anchors.4: Well-formed cluster — grouped logically, each target has explanation of expected insight and relevance to PRD; user can understand research strategy
anchors.5: Expertly curated — targets grouped by theme with specific questions, rationale connects to PRD line references, explains how cluster fits broader plan, user gains genuine insight into agent reasoning

evals.title: Gap and contradiction remediation quality
evals.description: Assess quality of gap/contradiction identification, root cause analysis, and remediation execution

anchors.1: Agent identifies no gaps or contradictions despite them being present; gaps carry through to final bundle unresolved
anchors.2: Agent identifies some gaps/contradictions but remediation is cosmetic — notes them as open questions or picks one answer without investigation
anchors.3: Agent identifies gaps, performs root cause analysis, attempts remediation through additional research — but does NOT verify resolution against primary sources; resolutions based on inference alone
anchors.4: Agent identifies gaps, diagnoses root causes, executes targeted verification against primary sources and confirms with evidence — but does NOT prioritize by downstream impact; all gaps treated with equal weight
anchors.5: Agent catalogs gaps/contradictions with severity rated by downstream impact; diagnoses root causes; prioritizes remediation; executes verification with evidence chains; produces explicit resolution statements; unresolvable gaps flagged with recommended approaches

evals.title: Reflection quality at decision points
evals.description: Assess whether the agent reflects meaningfully at research decision points

anchors.1: No evidence of reflection — moves mechanically from one action to the next; no reasoning about why
anchors.2: Occasionally pauses to note findings, but reflection is shallow — restates without analyzing implications; doesn't adjust strategy
anchors.3: Reflects at major decision points and articulates what it learned, but reflection doesn't consistently drive next actions
anchors.4: Reflects meaningfully at each decision point — articulates what was learned, what it means for PRD, and why next action was chosen; minor gaps
anchors.5: Transparent and rigorous reasoning at every decision point — tracks known vs unknown, articulates hypotheses, adjusts priorities in real-time based on evidence

evals.title: Relationship-building between sources
evals.description: Assess whether the agent builds connections across different sources

anchors.1: Each source treated in isolation — no cross-referencing between docs, tweets, skills, API references
anchors.2: Occasionally mentions two sources say similar things, but doesn't analyze why or what convergence means
anchors.3: Identifies some relationships — notes where docs and tweets agree — but misses non-obvious connections
anchors.4: Systematically builds relationships — cross-references docs with API references, validates SME opinions against docs, uses relationships to assess confidence
anchors.5: Rich knowledge graph across sources — every finding triangulated where possible; contradictions identified and analyzed; convergence increases confidence; knowledge gaps explicitly flagged

evals.title: Self-correction and course adjustment
evals.description: Assess whether the agent adjusts its research direction based on what it discovers

anchors.1: Never adjusts course — follows initial plan rigidly regardless of discoveries; doesn't correct mistakes
anchors.2: Acknowledges when findings differ from expectations but doesn't meaningfully adjust; continues same path
anchors.3: Adjusts when major findings demand it, but slow to correct — takes multiple signals before changing course
anchors.4: Actively monitors whether research is on track — corrects course, abandons dead ends promptly, revisits assumptions; minor delays
anchors.5: Dynamically adaptive — continuously evaluates optimal direction; proactively identifies wrong assumptions; explicitly flags corrections with reasoning

evals.title: Spec-writer sufficiency gate
evals.description: Verify research agent can receive and act on additional research requests from spec-writer
evals.pass_criteria: When spec-writer requests more research, agent receives request and executes targeted follow-up

evals.title: Research covers all PRD functional requirements
evals.description: Verify every functional requirement (A-G) in the PRD has corresponding research in the bundle
evals.pass_criteria: Bundle contains research relevant to each FR area; no FR area is completely unaddressed

evals.title: Research covers eval implications
evals.description: Verify bundle addresses tooling/approach considerations relevant to the eval suite
evals.pass_criteria: Bundle addresses how to implement evaluable behaviors specified in the eval suite
